title: Jeffrey Carney
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Jeffrey Carney
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

education_entries: Ph D in Organic Chemistry, University of Notre Dame
research_interests: My research interests cover several areas of organic chemistry, including heterocyclic chemistry, cyclization reactions, cascade (or tandem) reactions, catalysis, reaction methodology development, and total synthesis. We are also interested in green chemistry and medicinal chemistry projects.
teaching_interests: My teaching interests are organic chemistry, synthetic chemistry, and retrosynthetic analysis. I also enjoy biological mechanisms and transformations, pharmacology/medicinal chemistry, and communication and critical thinking in chemistry.
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2019). Convergent total synthesis of (-)-rubriflordilactone B and (-)-pseudo-rubriflordilactone B . Angewandte Chemie, International Edition/Wiley. Volume, 58. Issue, 50. Pages, 18177-18181.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2017). Anhydrous Monoalkyl Guanidines in Aprotic and Nonpolar Solvents: Models for Deprotonated Arginine Side Chains in Membrane Environments . ACS Omega. Volume, 2. Issue, 10. Pages, 7239-7252.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2014). Influence of Soil Cadmium and Mycorrhizal Fungi on the Fatty Acid Profile of Flax (Linum usitatissimum) Seeds . J. Sci. Food Agric.. Volume, 95. Issue, 12. Pages, 2528-2532.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2014). Retrosynthetic Rummy - A Synthetic Organic Chemistry Card Game . Journal of Chemical Education. Volume, 92. Issue, 2. Pages, 328-331.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2014). Comprehensive Approach to the Development of Communication and Critical Thinking: Bookend Courses for Third- and Fourth-Year Chemistry Majors . Journal of Chemical Education. Volume, 91. Issue, 10. Pages, 1649-1654.
awards_honors: Provost's Award for Excellence in Teaching Student Writing, Provost and The Writing Council. (2014)

Presentations: Rhiannon Canfield, Ronald Quinlan, Jeffrey Carney, Dmitry Liskin, Andrew Higgs, , Abigail Brehm, Andrew Brockway, Ronald Quinlan, SERMACS 2024, "Simple Amine Protection Reaction as a Learning Tool in Organic, Analytical Undergraduate Laboratories.," Atlanta, GA. (2024)
Grants and Sponsored Research: Jeffrey Carney, Margaret McEwan, "Toward the Total Synthesis of Torreyunlignans," Sponsored by Pfizer Inc.
